title: Meibomian gland dysfunction

investigations.text: Supports dry-eye symptom screening, visual symptoms, tear-film tests, fluorescein or lissamine staining, and cornea, conjunctiva, or eyelid-margin observation without inferring diagnosis from symptoms alone.
investigations.rationale: Supports dry-eye symptom, tear-film, staining and eyelid-margin assessment for meibomian-gland dysfunction; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports dry-eye symptom screening, visual symptoms, tear-film tests, fluorescein or lissamine staining, and cornea, conjunctiva, or eyelid-margin observation without inferring diagnosis from symptoms alone.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Symptoms, duration, gland plugging and expression, lid margin, tear film, staining, contact lenses, diagnosis, treatment and response are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for meibomian-gland dysfunction.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
